name: Hodge Bank & Trust Company
description_short: Hodge Bank & Trust Company is a full-service bank offering personal and business banking products including checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with FDIC protection.
description_long:
Hodge Bank & Trust Company operates as a traditional community bank with an online presence at hodgebank.net. The bank has been in operation long enough to maintain multiple physical locations and a full suite of banking services, though specific founding date and history details are not publicly available on their website.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, and personal loans. On the business side, Hodge provides commercial loans, real estate financing, and consumer credit products. They also issue credit cards and maintain a trust company division (reflected in their name). Additional services include safe deposit boxes, online banking, debit/ATM access, telephone banking, and financial calculators to help customers with planning.

Hodge Bank distinguishes itself through traditional relationship banking with physical branch locations, complemented by modern digital conveniences like online banking and bill pay. They provide direct access to third-party financial tools (Kelly Blue Book, NADA guides, Experian credit reports) and maintain FDIC insurance coverage up to $250,000 per account category. The bank emphasizes accessibility through multiple contact methods including a 1-800 phone number and community involvement.

As a regional community bank, Hodge Bank serves customers seeking traditional banking with local branch presence. However, the website provides limited detail about specific loan terms, interest rates, fees, or competitive advantages. Customers should contact the bank directly via phone (1-800-500-1044) or visit a branch location for detailed product information, pricing, and qualification requirements. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC deposit insurance protection up to $250,000 per account category
Multiple physical branch locations for in-person banking
Comprehensive product suite including personal loans, business loans, and real estate financing
Online banking with bill pay and check reordering capabilities
Trust company services available through subsidiary division
Safe deposit box services for document and valuables storage
cons: Website lacks specific information on interest rates, APRs, and fee schedules
Limited transparency about loan qualification requirements and terms
No online application process visible or linked from website
Minimal details about competitive advantages versus other regional banks
About Us page appears to be non-functional (404 error)
best_for: Local residents near Hodge Bank branch locations seeking relationship banking
Small business owners needing commercial loans and business banking services
Customers who prefer traditional banks with physical branches over online-only institutions
diagnosis: Hodge Bank & Trust Company is best for individuals and small businesses in their service area seeking traditional community banking with physical branch locations and FDIC protection. Primary caveat: the website provides minimal pricing and product detail, requiring customers to contact the bank directly to evaluate specific terms, rates, and fees.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
